title: Google Play Services in Recent News
description: Reports from MakeUseOf and 9to5Google highlight battery life observations and storage policy changes affecting Android users.

sources.title: I turned off one Google app and gained nearly two hours of battery life
All Android backup data now counts toward your Google Account storage [Update: Email]
Google makes big change on Android smartphones

sections.heading: Battery Life Observations
paragraphs: MakeUseOf reported that turning off one Google app resulted in gaining nearly two hours of battery life.

sections.heading: Android Backup Changes
paragraphs: 9to5Google reported that all Android backup data now counts toward Google Account storage. Additionally, MyBroadband reported that Google made a big change on Android smartphones.

sections.heading: Search Interest Data
paragraphs: According to the provided facts, the search term 'Google Play Services' had a reported search lower bound of 500 in the US region.

faq.q: What did MakeUseOf report regarding battery life?
faq.a: MakeUseOf reported that turning off one Google app resulted in gaining nearly two hours of battery life.

faq.q: What change did 9to5Google report regarding Android backups?
faq.a: 9to5Google reported that all Android backup data now counts toward Google Account storage.

Google makes big change on Android smartphones

review.notes: rules: all rules checks passed | reviewer(qwen-max): The article is well-structured and accurately reflects the provided facts. Attribution to sources is clear and consistent. The only minor issue is that the MyBroadband source is mentioned in the draft but the specific change it reported is not detailed, which slightly affects factuality.
